title: Smoky Dense Vietnamese Pork Braise
description: A rich and adventurous Vietnamese dish featuring pistachio paste, slow-cooked to perfection in a smoky braise. This paleo-friendly recipe is perfect for a special occasion or a cozy night in.
instructions: In a small bowl, mix together pistachio paste, fish sauce, coconut aminos, smoked paprika, and black pepper.
In a large Dutch oven, heat the coconut oil over medium-high heat. Sear the pork shoulder until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the pot and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the pistachio paste mixture to the pot and stir to coat the pork.
Cover the pot with a lid and transfer to the preheated oven.
Braise the pork for 2 1/2 hours, or until tender and easily shredded with a fork.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Shred the pork with two forks and stir to coat with the smoky sauce.
Serve the pork hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs and a side of cauliflower rice or roasted vegetables.
